notes: This skill writes to live Google Sheets CRMs, so functional evals are run by Aleem/Claude via --dry-run rather than by subagents writing to production. These prompts are primarily for description-trigger testing and as a record of intended behavior.

evals.prompt: I just finished scraping a fresh batch of agency owners into the Instant Instagram Leads sheet. Push the new ones into the CRM with messages.
evals.expected_output: Triggers leads-to-crm. Runs push.py --channel instagram (dry-run first), classifies rows, generates Touch 1 messages for genuinely new handles, appends to the Instagram Outreach CRM, stamps source rows Added.

evals.prompt: Run the linkedin push for me
evals.expected_output: Triggers leads-to-crm. Runs push.py --channel linkedin: reads Instant LinkedIn Leads, dedups by /in/ slug, pushes new leads to the LinkedIn Outreach CRM with connection notes.

evals.prompt: The instagram CRM has a bunch of duplicate rows from earlier runs, can you clean it up?
evals.expected_output: Triggers leads-to-crm. Runs push.py --channel instagram --dedup (dry-run first) to remove duplicate rows by handle, keeping the first occurrence.

evals.prompt: How do I get the new facebook leads I scraped into a CRM the same way as instagram?
evals.expected_output: Triggers leads-to-crm. Explains the add-a-channel steps (new Channel subclass in channels.py with identity functions + register in CHANNELS), since Facebook is the intended next extension.
